note: The portable honest-DOM conformance suite: language-agnostic cases every implementation (domx, stateless, and future spokes) must pass. A case names a public function and its arguments; when the result is an extractor (a higher-order shortcut), applyTo is the element it reads. Cases are data; the runner resolves the function, applies it, and deep-compares the result. Elements are plain objects, so the property-read shortcuts need no DOM; attr:/data: are covered by the unit tests, which can carry method mocks.
